Forza horizon 6 was just weeb bait. it worked they have 161k peak of players.
was it really worth 5 years? 5 years for this. Forza horizon 4 still remains to be the last good forza horizon game to date. the whole series changed fh1 was a completely different franchise at this point

without a doubt, the best Forza Horizon ever available on steam - or at least it was, unfortunately. hopefully that changes again soon with the new entry

FH5 may have better graphics, physics and deeper customization, but in almost every other aspect, FH4 still stands out. The soundtrack is better (even though I personally spent most of my time listening to Horizon XS), and the British setting delivers an atmosphere that feels truly unique
Even with a smaller map than FH5, drivng across Great Britain felt far more immersive and memorable.


the expansions were also handled much better,  while H4 had more fun dlc, its successor often gives the impression of rushed and recycled content. This becomes even more noticeable in the missions, since many of FH5’s activities eventually feel repetitive and less engaging

another highlight was the “business” missions, which offered a great balance between challenge and fun. On top of that, earning all 3 stars in the five businesses rewarded players with a passive income that could exceed 2 million in-game credits per month

I understand that Forza Horizon is designed as an arcade racing game rather than a simulator, and everyone has their own way of enjoying it - Still, here’s my recommendation: try disabling all driving assists - including stability control (STM), traction control (TCS), use manual transmission with clutch, enable simulation steering, and maybe even turn off the racing line and ABS while increasing drivatar difficulty. It may feel painful at first, but the experience becomes far more rewarding and immersive. taking a nighttime drive across the map with those settings while listening to relaxing music is simply magnificent

It’s a beautiful game, and for anyone passionate about cars, just like I am, Horizon 4 is essential. Enjoy your time in Japan, and have a great trip everyone.
I cannot sign in on linux because microslop demands that i sign in with a security key instead of a email and password like every other ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sign in in every other video game

One of the best arcade racing games.
The map of Great Britain is stunningly beautiful, and the changing seasons add a fresh touch.
I enjoyed playing it. It's just a relaxing little g

I've wanted to play this game for years, after seeing the trailer at E3 2018. Especially because I've actually traveled to Edinburgh, Scotland and the surrounding countryside so this was such a cool setting for me personally. 

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3733796806

I tried the demo but at the time it was on the Windows store only and that experience sucked massively, so I never bought it. Once it hit steam and got a heavy discount I finally snatched it before it was delisted. 

One massive negative for me: trying to use a Logitech wheel SUCKS SO BAD in this game, it's legit almost unplayable. Cars oversteer and spin out just twitching the wheel, and I tried so many different setting tweaks or even custom car tunes. Controller really is the intended way to play, which sucks as I wanted to use my wheel. 

Besides this, the game is gorgeous, has fantastic music, and has so many awesome cars. I was so stoked to see the Cyberpunk Quadra car, which I found totally by accident. Still looks and runs incredible for an older game now.

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3733796386

Excellent car game, made even more special for me as I've been to the exact setting this game takes place in.

This game is really good to play after your had like an enough 9-5....since it was delisted I would really still like to buy the rest of the DLC's ....why delist them....you guys could've had the DLCS for people to buy them later after purchasing the main game (even in terms of the main game being delisted)...but this game still holds good till date even in comparison with FH5(I've played those on consoles)

Unfortunately, the game is currently almost unplayable for me. Either it refuses to launch at all, or it crashes/freezes during gameplay, often forcing a hard PC restart. I’ve tried multiple fixes, but the stability is terrible. Very disappointing considering how great the game itself is.
the game no longer works. crashes a few minuets after starting.
The game is overall very good, graphics are good quality, there are't many bugs in the game, the game is very smooth and i recommand this game to all car lovers.

Its a banger arcade racer game with still a beautiful graphics. there are still a lot of racers driving online. 
just sad that its not available to purchase anymore

I can't deal with this ♥♥♥♥. I start the game logged in and through my xbox account. The game still asks me to log in again inside the game. I use phone verification. Then i am asked to further secure account with email. So i put my email in. IT'S REJECTED. Because it's already my account email and it actually wants an additional 2nd email? 
No existing ignore button. I close the promt and get rejected from playing the game I have payed for.

2 emails and a phone number is too much to demand to just play a game. during this entire thing a narrator is playing speech to text, despite narrator being off in the OS settings. Really irritating.

Maybe my account is bugged because it's old, but that is still a microslop bug. And requiring a log in to begin with, is asking alot! 

I don't hate this game, i used to play it back in the day on console. But the xbox/Microslop experience is hell, atleast on computer.

By far my favorite FH game and the best one in my opinion (though i didn't get to play FH3 yet).
It runs really well (ultra-extreme mix ~200FPS @4k), looks good, progression is saner than in 5 and the map is fun to drive around. fortune island kinda breaks the economy but it's whatever.

it goes without saying that forza is amazing when it comes to graphics and how the driving feels but the most bullshi thing in any game is the forza drift zones whoever designed them probably doesnt play the game or hasnt tried the drift zones after designing them
Super fun casual open world racing, too bad it's delisted.

“Pure driving joy wrapped in one of the most beautiful open worlds ever made.”

Forza Horizon 4 is the kind of game that makes you fall in love with driving—even if you’re not usually into racing games. It’s not about strict realism or punishing difficulty; it’s about freedom, speed, and fun.

Set in a stunning recreation of the UK, the world feels alive thanks to its dynamic seasons system. Spring, summer, autumn, and winter don’t just change visuals—they completely transform how cars handle, how roads feel, and how you approach events. It keeps the experience constantly fresh.

The driving itself is smooth, accessible, and incredibly satisfying. Whether you’re drifting through country roads, racing supercars through cities, or just cruising and listening to in-game radio stations, everything feels polished and responsive.

Content-wise, there’s a huge amount to do. Story missions, races, stunts, online events, seasonal challenges—it’s easy to get lost for hours without even noticing.

That said, it leans heavily toward accessibility, so hardcore sim racing fans might find it too forgiving. Also, the progression can feel a bit relaxed compared to more structured racing games.

Pros:

Gorgeous open world with dynamic seasons
Extremely fun and accessible driving physics
Tons of content and events
Great variety of cars and customization

Cons:

Not very challenging for sim racing fans
Progression can feel too casual
Online elements sometimes inconsistent

Verdict:
One of the best arcade racing experiences ever made. If you just want to drive and have fun, this game absolutely delivers.

Recommended 👍

Game runs okish
tho it like to crash under newer Proton versions...
At least its better than FH5
also please fix the performance issues with the targethardware.dll file!

Forza Horizon 4 is a fun arcade racer and open world sandbox, when it works. I signed out one single time to try and fix a few online server connection issues. The horrors beyond my comprehension that followed were some of the most irritating steps I've ever had the displeasure of taking to get a game up and running again. The log-in screen is mind-bogglingly bugged, showing a blank white screen, which required me to run a custom BAT script to remove Xbox credentials, sign into Halo Infinite (A free xbox PC game), kill the Forza Webhelper process with task manager, AND finally, press tab+enter incredibly fast to get the sign-in screen to load up. Please fix this. It is downright atrocious.

They took it off the store, but they couldn't take it off the road. Servers are still packed, the UK seasons still hit different, and drifting through Edinburgh never gets old. Coming back to this masterpiece feels like home, and i think i'll still do it even after Forza Horizon 6 fully releases.
 Permanent install.

It is an arcade game.

Cars are indestructible . Even you turn off cosmetic damage option, your car never be totalled , only affecting the speed of the car .

Jump from a cliff? Your car suspension lives .

I never know why every studio design every car, even general public level brand like Mazda, can floor to 100km/hr easily, and then easily went to 200km/hr, but this is that type of arcade game. 

Don't ask me if it is like the real world, you need to turn more than a circle for the steering wheel for sharp turn, how can you do that in the game with controller or Logitech sim wheel, and make that turn easily. It just by magic, except you did need to have certain "lower" speed for some turn. Don't try it above 50km/hr on real world for almost every single in-game counterpart of turn and corner.

So that for some people , the "D" class race my be more realistic and actually more competitive , but for "S" class race, it just magic verse magic.

Also, the suggested route thing can still turn on in PVP. So that skill is not that demanding, knew how to tune the car (or just download someone else tuning) may be more important.

(edit: i forgot the part that use drifting (the arcade kind) may have advantage in winning anyway, but too much would just make the game drifting game, not racing game)


if you like NFS, or Just Cause like kill time car arcade , it may be your cup of tea. It just delisted in the shop anyway.

But if you want more realistic , this may be not the game you want.


edit: this game does not support PS4 controller natively (as it is Xbox sort of exclusive) , just use Steam input to map your controller

What a beautifuil has game, man this game lowkey made me sexy in body and in mind growing up playing Forza 2 and 4. Like im secretly into cars still, and a wizard at it because i played these games.

Not really into cars games, as these games I only played when i was a kid before getting my driving lisncese, but who knows I might get the new one which takes in Japan which is fire.

The most worthless racing game I have ever had the misfortune of playing.
Don't get me wrong racing and customization of cars is fun, but the rest of it is just plain pathetic.
From the forced radio, and mandatory keybinds (Why is "Next Radio" bind mandatory, but not Gear Up/Down? In a racing game.) to the beyond annoying request of linking with Steam. If I didn't do it on the first login, I am as sure as hell not gonna do it on the 10th.
Good game but lots of bugs I am facing recently.

You can call me a sim racing snob, but after quite some time I took this game up when it was about to be delisted, and I've never had so much fun, not in Assetto, not in Project cars, no where. 
I like this game a lot.

My microsoft account was hacked, could no longer access the online mode. I signed in from a new xbox account, and i dont see the ultimate edition dlc in it. Microsoft wouldnt help with account recovery. I dont get the DLC in my new account, whats the point of the purchase then. Wish i could get a refund, or the dlc in my new account. Moreover wish that microsoft was even a little bit efficient as Steam in terms of account recoveries. This game is no more available for purchase. Very disappointed.
An absolute killer game with amazing land scapes of England and Europe along with great cars.

Peel P50 is the best car ever made. 

Even as this game is dead as i'm writing this, there are still a TON of singleplayer things to do, and i've got hundreds of hours worth of races to do, and its a real shame they killed off this game. L move Michaelsoft.

Glad i got this on the day of it's funeral.
This game is literally broken lmao. It crashed my pc. I don't have a beefy pc, but ♥♥♥♥ man

Please describe what you liked or disliked about this game and whether you recommend it to others.
Please remember to be polite and follow the Rules and Guidelines.

The optimization is crazy compare to modern game nowadays, didnt even expect running at 1600x900 resolution with custom medium setting 30 fps smoothly on vega 8(intergrated graphics), and since it basically carried my childhood add even more point. Well done, developers. Despite this game has been unlisted, it still keeping up with modern video games. What a beautiful game.

Amazing gameplay - High attention rate, the best part i like about was able to purchase new cars with the help of Auction and further i loved the realistic graphics - more bright and cars has metallic looks on for Forza Horizon 4, unlike the FH 5 and new FH 6.

Not sure how FH5 and FH6 goona be, but am more into - what FH4 was offering, feedback on reason being what my opinions stand straight for the new updates like these are: FH5 graphics are not attractive - but gameplay improvement is there, And as far as FH6 graphics is good - but gameplay lacks too much.
It is a fun driving and racing game. So many cars of different classes. Challenging races.

Servers are a bit iffy. The AI rubber bands and can launch you off courses. The graphics are showing slight age in 2026. The game is delisted from steam. 
But in you have it in your library, it is still a gem. Servers are always full, people always challenging you to races, 2 great expansions, fun engaging gameplay loop, and after finishing the latest Forza Horizon 6, i find this game has more charm.
So if you still own it and it's DLC on steam, you own a gem, if not, i would go look for a physical copy for consoles as it is still worth playing in 2026
The perfect game to just mute the in game music, put on your own playlist and drift around the british countryside for hours. 10/10
It's a blast after playing Forza 6 but I still need to come back and give a review for Forza 4. My first Forza game that bring me to the franchise, literally fall in love...

I managed to grab Forza Horizon 4 before it was pulled from the Steam store, and I'm glad I did. Being a Forza Horizon title, it delivers exactly what you'd expect: a solid, enjoyable racing experience.
The two expansions, Fortune Island and LEGO Speed Champions, feel more like optional extras than essential additions. I wouldn't have felt like I was missing out had they not been included.
Overall, it's a good game, but one you can comfortably skip. If you're jumping into the series for the first time, you'd likely get more out of starting with Forza Horizon 5 or 6.

This game have a place on my heart with FH3, these games are peak on xbox, and having it on pc its just the best thing ever.
8/10 Vehicles
7/10 Physics
7/10 Graphics
8/10 Optimization
Total : 30/40 My childhood xbox game with FH3 !

Forza Horizon 4 is honestly one of the most iconic racing games I’ve played. I spent most of my hours on Game Pass and it was my first Horizon game, so it really stuck with me. The map, especially the seasons, is what makes it special.
Seems to be an alright game. Nothing too special for me, really. But it's still recommended. Good graphics and an alrightish optimization.
I think it's good overall, but I think the map lack of some scenery or I just do not prefer London road that much. The lego valley is very interesting.
great game the graphics is amazing 100% recommended
i have played fh4 over Microsoft Store, i have many hours on that game, and i love it, Really, England is good to explore.

(9/10)

Forza Horizon 4 is pure freedom on four wheels.

This game isn’t just about racing. It’s about atmosphere, exploration, music, seasons, beautiful roads, and that feeling of escaping reality for a while. Even after dozens of hours, driving around the UK map still feels relaxing and magical.

And honestly… I’m really glad I bought this game before it got removed from digital stores.

Because this game deserves to be preserved.

The map is absolutely gorgeous 🌄
The recreation of Britain is stunning — countryside roads, villages, forests, highways, rainy weather, autumn leaves, snowy mountains, and city streets all blend together perfectly. Every season completely changes the mood of the world:

Autumn feels warm and cinematic 🍂
Winter turns the map into controlled chaos ❄️
Spring feels calm and colorful 🌱
Summer is pure festival energy ☀️

Driving itself feels amazing. Whether you’re casually cruising at sunset or flying at 300 km/h through narrow roads while barely avoiding traffic, the game constantly feels fun.

And the car variety is insane.

You can drive:

hypercars
classic muscle cars
rally monsters
JDM legends
off-road beasts
random meme vehicles
or a giant van at dangerous speeds because good decisions are optional 💀

The sound design, graphics, and weather effects still look incredible even years later. Sometimes I’d open the game with no intention of racing and just drive around listening to music like it was a virtual road trip simulator.

That’s when you know a racing game did something special.

The progression system is also refreshingly fun compared to grind-heavy racers. The game constantly rewards you with events, cars, wheelspins, challenges, and activities without making everything feel like work.

And unlike many modern racing games, Forza Horizon 4 actually feels alive.

Online free roam can create hilarious random moments with strangers, convoys with friends are genuinely fun, and seasonal events keep the world feeling active.

Of course, it’s not perfect:

Some races can feel repetitive
The online servers occasionally act possessed 👻
The LEGO DLC is either genius or insanity depending on your mood
And yes, you will accidentally spend hours tuning cars instead of racing them 😭

But overall?

This is easily one of the best arcade racing games ever made.

And honestly, there’s something bittersweet now knowing it got delisted. New players may never fully experience discovering this game during its peak years.

Lucky me though — I got to own and experience this beautiful game before it disappeared from stores 🚗✨

Verdict:
A gorgeous open-world racing experience with an unforgettable UK atmosphere, fantastic driving, incredible variety, and pure comfort-game energy.

Some games age.

Forza Horizon 4 became timeless. 🌌

Great game good fun, would rather purhcase this than Frorza5. I believe that this game has aged better than 5. Would reccomned trying out if anyone is put off by Forza6's sizeable current price tag. 7.5/10 would reccomned to other if they are curious but not a must play to those not a fan of racing games. But its time to retire to game and fly to Forza Horizon 6 this game and the last iteration will have a special place in my heart. To all reading this, it had a pretty good life and maybe will have a good life for years to come but after Forza Horizon 6 is out there wont be many players to find, but those you find in it are truly loyal and the best people out there. So 10/10 for me.

Not sure if it is the best one if you are looking for a simulator, but the drive feels realy good when you switch off the assistanses and use manual drive with clutch. That's the best way to play it in my opinion. So i recomend it to the racing / driving crowd.

its a great game sucks they have to delist it and the previous games Forza Horizon 4 was delisted from digital storefronts on December 15, 2024, because the licensing agreements for its vehicles, music, and certain real-world landmarks expired. Microsoft only holds the legal rights to feature this intellectual property for a set period. Once those contracts expire, the developer must stop selling the game.
Best game with sexy graphics, must play...

This game was my introduction into the Forza Horizon franchise. Loved it. Wished FH5 was more like this game. Hopefully FH6 is. 

It's been too long ago that I've played to mention in detail what specifically was enjoyable, but I have fond memories of tearing through hillside meadows among towering wind turbines and racking-up style points. Also the opponent AI was scaled for the match, not the player -- which made a few rounds pretty fun. I think I enjoyed the competitive night time races the most. The sound track slapped on those.
amazing game can run on high on 40-50 fps which is not great but on high its ok

Once you turn off the giga cringe dialoge, which has carried over to 5 and 6, this game really is a polished pleasure to play.
I played 5 a little and the new iteration a little bit on XBOX, it seems like a map update mostly. the game systems were founded here and have only been improved since. 
Performance is great, this has been optimised to hell and back.
I know I'm using hardware that came out way newer than the game, but it runs great maxed out at 4k native. Not a stutter in sight and the game is fun as well.
Very good game worth trying and it runs very smoth
Cant afford or run Forza Horizon 6 on my PC so gonna play Forza Horizon 4 but why the heck is it winter in UK when its summer. bbbrrrrr.

FH4 is a good game and there is a "BUT"

I originally returned to FH4 because I was preparing to buy Forza Horizon 6 and wanted to revisit the series first.

Instead, I spent my entire weekend and the following days dealing with CRASHES, online/profile synchronization issues, and missing liveries/skins. Although I manage to return to the road by reinstalled the game, this has seriously affected my confidence in buying FH6.

If this can happen in FH4, how can I be confident that FH6 will not have similar online/profile issues?
the seasonal events are fun and I like the voice actors too
great racing. My first ever car related game. visuals are amazing car models are great

I didn't play Forza before. It's my first one.
Right from the beginning you see that the game is really beautiful.
Different seasons of the year is a cool feature here.
The music is pretty good here.
There are a lot of cars too choose. If you have enough money the you can buy a supercar right away.
Drivers are going to "adapt" to your car level, so the race is kinda fair.
You are free to setup in-game difficulty for your pleasure.
It is the first game where I experienced driving with a manual transmission and I was shifting gears by myself!
You can customize your car and also you can choose prepared stylings from the community.
There is a giant open world with tons of activities you are free to do.
I would say that it is the biggest difference from other racing games I've played and one of the comments I saw on YouTube I think fits best here: "Freedom sounds good on paper, but structure gives motivation. When everything is optional, nothing is mandatory".
Still it was an interesting journey.
Great game, BUT I now need to sign in to Microsoft account to play! - That is a thumbs down; I just want to click and play (no microsoft xbox login, authenticator bla bla).

Farewell to the second best FH game behind 3 (before 6 has released). Although winter is hated on, the map looked good and played good in all seasons and the car roster was perfect. The introduction of star cards and Story's made it even better.

Forza Horizon 4 still clears 5 for me. the map just had more soul — driving through european roads at night with spotify on felt different.

some of my best gaming memories came from messing around with my bro for hours doing absolutely nothing important. honestly one of those games that’s more about the feeling than the racing.

if horizon 6 can bring that atmosphere back, we’re set.
Amazing game that made my dream come true, cars, beautifull landscaper, amazing graphics.
community is great and nice and overall is enjoyable 10/10

I have only 17 hours into this game, I really liked the game, but I stopped playing because life got too busy, but I was hoping to come back to play some day.  Now that Forza 6 came out recently I got the strong urge to play Forza 4 again.  Went to re-install it after the de-listing, but it crashes system every time no matter what troubleshooting.  I feel like they stole my money by killing it (if I had hundreds of hours in I'd be like "What is this guy complaining about?", but 17 feels like a ripoff and I wish I could have explored the world and vehicles.  This is a reason why I won't buy Forza 6, I feel like they will just kill it when 8 comes out (we don't own anything and we should be happy) in the future just like they did to Forza 4, and if I really liked that game world and they yanked it away again I'd be yearning to play something I could never have or own or see again; why get vested when you can't stay, or re-live things or find new things that you've missed, or pause to play later in life?  I never needed the multi-player feature, it was only for the pleasure of cruising in odd vehicles.  Microsoft XBox killing this, they are pricks, and they could've still made money on it by keeping it available, it wouldn't steal sales because it would make their world bigger (Britain-ish 4, Japan-ish 6 etc..)  Those who like Forza 6 would perhaps like to get into these other older versions; retro-play has been a thing for quite a while.  They don't have to run Software as a Service forever on a thing, just kill the multi-player if you don't want to support it; there are people out here who like to play just the AI's and/or cruise open worlds casually.   Also, it's kind of tragic to lose all that art, all those vehicles, that world!

Forza Horizon 4 was the game that got me through the pandemic. Every single day, I would wake up, do my online school work, then hop on my Xbox One to play this game for the rest of the night. This game is responsible for turning me into who I am today. If I had one chance to go back in time, I would use it to go back and play this game for the first time again. When I heard it was being taken off of the Xbox marketplace, I knew I absolutely needed to buy it. So that one day, maybe my children will be able to experience the same thing I did. Thank you Turn 10 Studios, for the greatest and most meaningful years of my life.
